rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to component ports.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE contracts, with type scopes for assumptions/guarantees and implementation scopes for bindings.
generic_rules: For component type contracts, place input assumptions and output guarantees only when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided.
Write output dependency guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Use helper declarations to align types in AGREE expressions with AADL port types.
Do not encode AADL properties like timing or unit tokens as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
Use eq declarations for boolean range checks: eq name : bool = value >= min and value <= max;
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean domain values into integers unless the port is explicitly an AGREE bool in scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing-related AADL tokens
Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent path usage in type contracts
